channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Takeaways from Trump's move to send Venezuelan migrants in the US to a prison in El Salvador
item.description: President Donald Trump has invoked a rarely used 1798 law that gives a president immense powers to arrest and deport noncitizens in a time of war.
item.dc:creator: Tim Sullivan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Takeaways from the Senate budget vote
item.description: The political battle lines are drawn for a debate in Washington and beyond over a Republican budget plan that’s a cornerstone of President Donald Trump’s domestic agenda.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Democrats propose bill to prevent Trump from relocating federal agencies outside DC
item.description: A cohort of Democratic representatives and senators are proposing legislation aimed at stalling President Donald Trump's efforts to relocate federal agencies outside of Washington, D.C., something the president has taken steps to start doing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate GOP approves framework for Trump's tax breaks and spending cuts after late-night session
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senate Republicans plugged away overnight and into early Saturday morning to approve their multitrillion-dollar tax breaks and spending cuts framework, hurtling past Democratic opposition toward what President Donald Trump calls the “big, beautiful bill” that's central to his agend
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, LEAH ASKARINAM and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's what happened during Trump's 11th week in office
item.description: President Donald Trump marked the week unveiling an unprecedented wave of tariffs on imports to the U.S., aligning with his position that countries have long taken advantage of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The frenzied 24 hours when Venezuelan migrants in the US were shipped to an El Salvador prison
item.description: It was just a few sentences in a meandering, hourlong presidential speech on a Friday afternoon. Along with talk about falling egg prices and a vow to expel “corrupt forces” from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: TIM SULLIVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In the race to save lives after the Myanmar quake, US rescuers are notable by their absence
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Day after day, Chinese rescue teams haul children and elderly people from collapsed buildings as cameras beam the thanks of grateful survivors around the world. Russian medical teams show off field hospitals erected in a flash to tend the wounded.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Things to know about the US Mine Safety and Health Administration and the coal industry
item.description: CHARLESTON, W.Va. (AP) — The U.S. Mine Safety and Health Administration is among the federal agencies selected for spending cuts by the Department of Government Efficiency. Nearly three dozen MSHA offices would have their leases terminated if the plans come to fruition.
item.dc:creator: JOHN RABY and LEAH WILLINGHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump Labor secretary hits the road as tariffs shock the markets
item.description: Many economists say the Trump administration’s global tariff strategy could spur a recession at home.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Sec Hegseth to visit Panama after Trump's demands for canal's return
item.description: Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth will visit Panama next week for meetings with the country's leaders, following remarks from President Donald Trump about potentially acquiring the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IRS cutting its workforce by 25%, eliminating agency's civil rights office
item.description: The Trump administration is continuing to shrink the Internal Revenue Service (IRS), as many employees are slated to be let go this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Interior secretary orders national parks to be open and accessible as workforce is cut
item.description: Under criticism for staff cuts across the country, Interior Secretary Doug Burgum is directing national parks to “remain open and accessible” and says officials will ensure proper staffing to do so.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth is to travel to Panama for meetings following Trump's demands for the canal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth will travel to Panama next week to meet with leaders of the Central American country following recent Trump administration allegations of Chinese interference in the operations of the critical Panama Canal shipping lane.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI Director Patel leads charge to root out Chinese influence on US soil
item.description: With Republicans sounding the alarm over Chinese-owned farmland in the U.S., FBI Director Kash Patel has named eradicating CCP influence a top priority at the bureau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Energy Department identifies thousands of nonessential positions at risk of DOGE cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Energy Department has identified thousands of federal workers it deems "nonessential” and would not be protected if there is another round of large-scale firings, according to a document obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bernie Sanders, Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez to visit Bakersfield as part of 'Fighting Oligarchy' tour
item.description: Vermont Sen. Bernie Sanders and New York Rep. Alexandria Ocasio Cortez are scheduled to make an appearance in Bakersfield as part of their "Fighting Oligarchy" tour.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to give TikTok another 75-day extension
item.description: President Trump said Friday that he plans to give TikTok another 75-day extension as his administration nears a deal to avert a ban on the popular social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICE arrests 37 in Washington roofing company raid
item.description: The company's CEO said those who were detained worked for the company for years as “tax-paying employees.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Here's how newly imposed tariffs will affect California products
item.description: President Donald Trump’s widespread tariffs could significantly impact California’s exports, such as automobiles, computer equipment and semiconductors, according to a study from the Public Policy Institute of California.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thune faces GOP divisions on critical budget resolution
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) faces a number of divisions among Senate Republicans that could derail the Senate budget resolution, a measure that will be critical to passing President Trump’s legislative agenda later this year.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: States sue to block Trump’s election order, saying it violates the Constitution
item.description: Democratic officials in 19 states are suing over President Donald Trump’s attempt to reshape elections across the U.S., calling it an unconstitutional invasion of states’ clear authority to run their own elections. Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison is one of the 19 officials suing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Michigan couple Paul, Christy Akeo freed from Mexico prison
item.description: Paul and Christy Akeo had been arrested in Cancún on March 4 after a high-end resort company accused them of fraud.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Coincidence? Recession-era artists releasing new music
item.description: "Recession pop" refers to the type of dance-floor jams that were popular around 2008 when the U.S. economy imploded.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: How the White House calculated reciprocal tariffs
item.description: President Trump triumphantly held up a poster board in the Rose Garden on Wednesday showing the reciprocal tariffs he plans to impose on nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Here's Gov. Joe Lombardo's plan to solve the Nevada housing crisis
item.description: A $250 million plan to spur construction of homes and apartments for low- to middle-income Nevadans is projected to provide homes for 16,000 people, according to officials who presented it Wednesday night in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Other DFL statewide officeholders break with Walz on return-to-office requirement
item.description: The attorney general, secretary of state and state auditor said they would keep existing telework rules in place because they are independent offices.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams to run as independent in mayoral election
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams, a Democrat, is planning to run as an independent in the 2025 mayoral election, he announced on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Democratic-backed Susan Crawford wins Wisconsin Supreme Court seat, cementing liberal majority
item.description: Susan Crawford, a Dane County judge who led legal fights to protect union power and abortion rights and to oppose voter ID, defeated Republican-backed Brad Schimel in a race that broke records for spending.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Yale professor leaving US: Trump launching 'brutal attack' on freedom
item.description: A professor at Yale University said Monday he would be leaving his post and the country over the Trump administration’s “brutal attack” on free speech.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘very angry’ and ‘pissed off’ at Putin during NBC News interview
item.description: President Donald Trump said he was “very angry” and “pissed off” when Russian President Vladimir Putin criticized the credibility of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s leadership, adding that the comments were “not going in the right location.
item.dc:creator: Kristin Welker, WFLA, NBC
